Wow! There's Royal Petal Herb over there too!
But it's a little far away...
Just in case, it might help if I grab it quickly, right?
Mel... do you feel a little better?
Yarrow alone won't bring down the fever...
...I have to go get Royal Petal Herb.
Cannot remove a placed statue during Battle.
The Corrupted Goddess who tries to hold everything in her grasp. It is said that just looking into her eyes makes one lose oneself and become a puppet.
Oh my, you've been talking about me however you like, haven't you? Sorry, but could you rewrite it?
Ah, my goddess. Please guide me. For you, I offer my body, my soul, everything.
Statue of the Goddess of Domination
A goddess statue with an unsettling smile. . . . Looking again, it seems like a truly beautiful smile.
Statue of the Goddess of Domination
A goddess statue with an unsettling smile. . . . Looking again, it seems like a truly beautiful smile.
An ornament imbued with the power of the Goddess of Domination
Flag of the Goddess of Domination
An unsettling flag. For some reason, more and more people have started using this flag.
My body feels heavy... This is hard...
I think I'm a little hurt.
There are Goblins on this road too...
I think I rested too long.
I should head back to the village before it gets late.
I can't waste any more time
I can do this... I can do this! They're just Goblins!
(Phew... I'm glad Mel is with me...)
Oh, right! Mel, are you feeling a bit better?
Hehe. That's a relief. Now let's do our best and find the village together!
Mel, shouldn't we go the other way?
Yeah. We shouldn't go this way.
Max HP, ATK, Spell Power, Attack Speed, Move Speed {0}%
(If I play dead, it'll pass by, right?)
Hey, I'm a nice Goblin. Don't hit me!
What are you looking at? Never seen a squirrel before?
(It glares while hiding the acorns behind its back.)
This is my territory! Go away!
S-Sorry! You can't go this way.
It looks like the Goblins are talking about dinner. D-Don't tell me I'm dinner?!
There's an herb farther down the road that helps reduce fever.
Eek! S-Sorry for speaking out of turn.
If you keep going straight, you'll reach Frontier Village.
You're in the way, so go back to the village now!
I never thought I'd see real Royal Petal Herb!
Hehe. I need to hurry and take it to Mel!
Mel, hang in there a little longer.
Wow...! Wow! This is Royal Petal Herb!
I feel like there's something nearby...
Should I look deeper into the forest?
The number of Pass Points you can buy increases as the event progresses.
Tired from one battle after another, Mel took off her Necklace for a moment to wash her face.
Mel's reflection in the lake showed how exhausted she was.
Without realizing it, Mel sat by the lakeside for a moment and drifted into a light sleep.
Treasure Chests are hidden all around the area.
Collect Treasure Chests to obtain rewards.
Log in for 10 days to get all Heroes!
Obtain Pass Points to increase Pass Level and claim rewards.
No… what happened to the village..
What happened here? The village... the people...!
There is no time, Mel. I will explain later, so get to safety first.
I have trained hard! And there are still people who could not get away.
... Where is Ian? If Ian were here, these guys would be no problem…
Ian must have joined the guards by now.
This old man will find the others, so do not worry and go now!
I cannot do that! I will fight with you too, Grandpa!
Mel... this is no time to be stubborn...
So there are still some resisting.
....."Hand over the 'Chosen One' to me, old warrior. If you do, I will spare your pitiful life."
The only thing I will give you is death!
It seems you do not value your life.
Mel, get out of here now!
I sense a very unpleasant aura from you.
No doubt. You are... the 'Chosen One' from the prophecy.
Heh heh heh... consider it an honor...
For I, Saras, Prince of the Void, will end your miserable fate with my own hands!

Song of Fire and Soul is a ritual tool used by Saeryung, a shaman from the East, and as its name suggests, it means the song of fire and soul. It is not just a weapon that controls flames. The flames born from Saeryung's song and spiritual power burn enemies and impurity, and have the power to protect allies and souls.

After losing her family to Vampires in the past, Neria formed the 'Red Moon' and now hunts down Vampires that have gone out of control, keeping peace in the border region of Nosferatu territory. But when she hunts Vampires, there is a strange madness in her smile.

Not enough Dimensional Remnants. Go to Dimensional Hero Summon?
In Dimensional Synchronization, you can use Dimensional Remnants to increase the Synchronization Level.
When the Synchronization Level increases, the Dimensional Hero's stats grow based on the level.
Dimensional Synchronization can be increased up to Level 10. The type and amount of stat increases vary by level.
The Dimensional Remnants required for Dimensional Synchronization increase with the level.
Dimensional Resonance is a system where the Original Hero and the Dimensional Hero share the same Level.
When the Original Hero's Level increases, the Dimensional Hero's Level also increases.
If you increase the Dimensional Hero's Level, the Original Hero's Level also increases.
One Original Hero can have multiple Dimensional Heroes. Even then, the Original Hero and all Dimensional Heroes share the same Level.
Use Dimensional Remnants to increase the Synchronization Level. The Dimensional Hero's stats increase based on the Synchronization Level.
If you Summon a duplicate of a Dimensional Hero you already own from Dimensional Hero Summon, you can Ascend it.
Ascend is available up to 5 stages.
Battle Start Skills are skills that Dimensional Heroes can use during Battle preparation.
Battle Start Skills have a round-based cooldown, and only 1 Battle Start Skill can be used each round.
Identical Battle Start Skills share the same cooldown.
Dimensional Heroes have a Battle Start Skill instead of an Awakened Ability, and it can only be used after the original Hero is Awakened.
A fully Ascended Dimensional Hero was Summoned again and has been converted into a Dimensional Core instead.
Ascend Hero <color=#FFD247>{0}</color> to Obtain a <color=#FFD247>Chroma Skin</color>!
Battle Start Skills are powerful special skills used by Dimensional Heroes before Battle begins.
To use a Battle Start Skill, you must meet these conditions. Original Hero Awakened Dimensional Hero Summoned and Deployed
If a Dimensional Hero that used a Battle Start Skill is removed from the Battlefield or unsummoned, the Battle Start Skill is canceled automatically.
Only 1 Battle Start Skill can be used per round.

After use, Battle Start Skills have a cooldown and cannot be used again for a set number of rounds. In the Temple of Challenge, each Battle counts as 1 round. In the Rift, each room Battle on the same floor counts as 1 round, and the cooldown resets when moving to a new floor. In the Arena, each Battle is treated as 1 round, so the cooldown applies. In single-round Battles such as Alliance Battle, Expedition, and Tower of Trials, Battle Start Skills can be used in every Battle without cooldown. On Defeat in Invasion, the cooldown does not apply. In Story Mode, these cooldown rules do not apply, and available Battles are set separately.
Not enough Dimensional Essence. Go to the Temple of Challenge Token Shop?
Dimensional Heroes cannot be deployed in the Sanctuary.
Drag the Hero you want to Ascend to select.

It awakens the killing instinct carved into the blood, granting a brief moment of immortality at the cost of reason. The moment the urge ends, even the last self holding on melts into Bloodthirst and disappears.

A gaunt Ophelia presides over the lords' council. The vassals look at her with disapproval.

Would it not be better for you to return to the Central region, my lady? You seem to need rest. It has already been decided that Lord Roderick's younger brother will take his seat, so please leave state affairs to him.
I have nowhere to return to. Where was Aldric during the last operation? If you intend to do as you please, then putting someone in that seat who never cared about this Dominion would suit you well.
Even if the former lord has passed away, that does not mean you may do as you please, my lady!
And you? Are you dishonoring Roderick before the dirt on his coffin has even dried? By his last will, full authority lies with me. Report the status of the military supplies.
Do you even understand what military supplies are, my lady?
Frostwald Dominion has long coexisted with the neighboring Vampires' territory.
The Vampires were a nuisance, seeing humans as nothing more than tasty food, but they could not be driven away completely.
Not only is each Vampire strong in combat, but the political and economic standing of their leader, House Nosferatu, is also firmly established.
Lord Roderick, known as the "Strategist," used skillful diplomacy to keep the Vampires neither too close nor too far away, reducing the victims among the people of his Dominion.
But now, Roderick is no longer in Frostwald Dominion.
You mean we can cut the budget here even more, Roderick? Hmm, I see.
She is not in her right mind.
The nobles whisper as they watch Ophelia dance in the banquet hall.
Is that Lady Ophelia? She is truly as beautiful as the rumors say. Will I get a chance to dance with Lady Ophelia?
Take a number and wait. I am only number ten myself, so do not try to cut in line.
She always acts like she is the only person in the world.
Oh, Ophelia? I cannot stand her. Still, I heard she will soon leave for the Duchy of Nuak.
Oh my, the North? Why would she go to such a cold place?
I hear it is a political marriage.
Oh... a political marriage... Do those still happen these days? Poor thing.
Wherever she was, Ophelia simply lived as things came.
Are there... balls in the North too?
Should I ask Father? No, better not...
Even after her political marriage to Roderick, Lord of Frostwald, her situation did not change.
How did such a pale noble lady end up all the way here?
They call it a political marriage, but really, she was sold off. Even after coming here, all she ever talks about is dresses and parties. What a headache she must have been.
I kind of feel bad for her... The lord does not even look in on her.
Hey, hey, we said not to worry about nobles. Do you know how much just one of her dresses costs? We would have to save for half a year!
Still... she looked cold last night. I should make the fire a little bigger.
My, what a beautiful jewel has fallen into a place like this. Won't you come with me? I will let you shine forever.
With the broken carriage behind him, the Vampire Vlad Nosferatu held out his pale hand to Ophelia.
Ophelia was on her way to yet another party she could no longer count, when she slowly lost consciousness inside the broken carriage.
When she opened her eyes, the place was beautiful. Magical lights flowed along the walls, and the ceiling shone like stars.
Vampires danced in the banquet hall, drank wine, and chatted happily.
To Ophelia, it was a sight that reminded her of the days when she shone at the balls in the capital.

My lady, this is "Gray Sunset," made from grapes from the Six Peaks of Kaldun. In the first sip, you taste ash. In the second, flowers. In the third, the stars. — A joke, of course. In truth, all three taste like grapes. Ha ha!
......It has been a long time since I met someone who takes wine so seriously.
It is an honor that you noticed. I care deeply for beautiful things. Wine, banquets, paintings, and beautiful women such as yourself.
Would you like a glass of wine?
I am fine. I will just go for a little walk.
Please, go have a look around the hall. If you stay only by my side, you may grow bored, no? Though for me, it would be an honor.
It is a pleasure to meet you. I have heard much about you from Lord Vlad. It must be hard to get used to the weather in the North, isn't it?
(It is much warmer here than at the Frostwald manor...) Not at all. The welcome here warms not only my heart, but my body as well.
Even each of your sentences is full of grace, my lady. I do hope you can enjoy this evening comfortably.
Thank you. (Sharp fangs, red eyes... Just as I thought, this place is...)
Oh, Lady Ophelia! It is lovely to see you!
A pleasure to meet you. What should I call you?
Please, just call me Bella. Lady Ophelia, you truly are beautiful! May I hope to see you dance tonight?
Ah, perhaps it still feels a little awkward. Our banquets keep going, so it does not have to be today. I would just love to see you dance someday, Lady Ophelia.
What do you mean, the banquet keeps going?
When someone new arrives, we keep the banquet going for several days. Ah, a party, an endless party! I loooove parties.
Roderick... If this place feels familiar and beautiful to me, is that wrong?
But there was no place for me in your Dominion, and here, I feel as if I am shining. Please, do not come looking for me.
Have you had a look around?
Lord Vlad, and everyone here... are you all "Vampires"?
Ah, please do not call us that. I do not much like the name "Vampire." It is not a very beautiful name. Look at me. Do I look like a monster? We are not so different from humans.

That someone like you would one day... fade in the cruel flow of time. An empty wineglass, a finished banquet, a ruined painting. And all living things die one day. When I think of that, it pains me greatly.
I can make you remain beautiful forever.
We call it the "Rite of Transformation." You would become part of our family.
Ophelia stayed at Vlad's manor and spent several dreamlike days there.
Little by little, swept away by Vlad's charming words and the endless parties, she forgot that she had been kidnapped and brought here.
And then came the day of another party she could no longer count.
Would you like a glass of wine?
(I do not know why I should not enjoy this. I do not know why I should not be happy....)
Ophelia decides to dance with Vlad beneath the chandelier.
Nothing could make me happier than being with you, my lady.
Did you say I could stay beautiful forever?
In that eternity... are there balls?
Yes. I would gladly share an eternal dance with you.
Have you ever seen the sun fall?
Vlad. I want to be with you.
Why are we gathered here today!
To stop any more victims. We must not only destroy the Vampires led by Vlad, but also rescue the Lady of Frostwald. That is why Lord Roderick stands with us today.
Thank you! If you rescue Ophelia, I promise that Frostwald Dominion will give the Red Moon its full support!
Lady Neria, the east guard changes in 3 minutes! The west in 5! Ah, and there are two bloodhounds by the north gate. The smell is awful if you even get a little close!
Yes, ma'am! I will report only the key points! I spotted Lady Ophelia at the banquet!
Um... But it looked like she was enjoying the party.
Vlad Moren is not on site!
So he is in his nest. Sola, rescue Lady Ophelia first before carrying out the operation. Your first priority is getting out safely.
Yes, ma'am! Rescue Lady Ophelia first! Then withdraw!
No. You must lead the troops.
To rescue Ophelia, only a small group, including Neria and Sola, enters the banquet hall through the back entrance.
If the Royal Vampires swarm in, this will get troublesome. We need to quickly deal with the vampires on the left and move on.
A lot of guards. Sola, head to the banquet hall first.
We will deal with all the vampires and follow after you.
Lady Neria, there are still some... who look human.
It is already too late! They are nothing but food for vampires now! Do not pity those who cannot be saved.
A corridor piled high with vampire corpses.
From afar, a blood-soaked Sola rushes over.
She ran off out of her mind! Toward Vlad Moren's manor!
And you came here instead of following her?
You told me before that if anything new happened, I should never act alone and report first...
Enough. I am going there. Red Moon! Full assault! Split up and fight!
Vlad's Dominion is thrown into chaos by the sudden Invasion.
Ophelia stumbles out of the banquet hall in confusion.
Die! Die! Because of you, my daughter...!
You filthy things, do you even know where you are...?
What is this? Am I in a nightmare? Vlad, where are you? Vlad!
As she runs in panic, Ophelia spots Vlad Moren.
Ah, there you are. Vlad! Vlad! I have been looking for you! What is all this...!
Vlad Moren was extremely agitated by the sudden battle and chaos.
The badly wounded Vlad Moren needed only one kind of nourishment.
Roderick appears, shoves Ophelia aside, and at the same time swings his sword at Vlad to drive him back.
Vlad's Blood Magic and Roderick's swordsmanship clash once, twice, three times.
Then a rushing fountain of blood tears off Roderick's sword arm.
A second spear of blood pierces Roderick's chest.
Neria comes out from inside the manor, too late.
Vlad Moren!!! You will not escape!
Vlad looks around, strikes down the pursuing Frostwald soldiers, and quickly moves away from the manor.
Roderick...? Roderick....? Why are you here? Why...?
Roderick came here himself to rescue you, Ophelia. He searched for you the whole two weeks you were gone.
Red Moon will not forget Roderick Frostwald.
After paying brief respect, Neria chases after Vlad.
Ophelia holds the fallen Roderick. His clothes are turning blood-red, but she does not care.
Why would you do this? You never cared for me.
To you, I was only worth that much. You did not have to risk your life!
I am just a foolish woman who only knows how to enjoy banquets.
Why do this now? What am I supposed to do now...?
Ophelia had only ever flowed where she was told, taking the shape of whatever vessel held her. But now the vessel is broken, and the wine called Ophelia does not know where to flow.
It has already been decided that Lord Roderick's younger brother will inherit the lordship, so leave state affairs to him.
If you mean to do as you please, then someone who never cared much for this Dominion would be a perfect fit.
By his final will, full authority is mine. Report the status of the military supplies.
I shall make you shine forever.
— I jest. In truth, all three taste like grapes. Haha!
Wine, banquets, paintings, and beautiful ladies such as yourself, my lady.
It must be hard to get used to the weather in the North, isn't it?
May we hope to see Lady Ophelia dance today?
Ah, parties, endless parties! I love parties sooo much.
Look at me. Do I look like a monster? We are not so different from humans.
And all living things must die someday. It pains my heart to think of it.
We must not only slay the Vampires led by Vlad, but also rescue Lady Frostwald.
Ah, and there are two bloodhounds by the north gate. They smell awful even if you get only a little closer!
Our first priority is getting out safely.
But now the cup is shattered, and the wine called Ophelia no longer knows where it should flow.
In the first sip, you taste ash; in the second, flowers; in the third, stars.
The hospitality here makes not only my heart, but my whole body feel warm.
The banquet will continue, so I do hope I can see Lady Ophelia dance another day, if not today.
Then please do not look for me.
A drained wineglass, a finished banquet, a ruined painting.
Lord Roderick stands with us for this.
(Every one of them has sharp fangs and red eyes... So this place really is...)
(Even if we deal with the weak ones too, there are only about fifteen in all...)
Tap the Battle Start Skill Altar on the right to use Neria's Battle Start Skill.
Use the Battle Start Skill and defeat the Vampire!
A wine named Ophelia does not know where to flow.

Born as the daughter of a Central noble house, Ophelia simply drifted through life, dancing in banquet halls and drinking wine. Even after her arranged marriage to Roderick, Lord of Frostwald, nothing changed. Then one day, after an Invasion, Ophelia is kidnapped to an unknown place.

The appearance rate for Dimensional Heroes is 0.5%, and increases by 4% per Summon starting from the 61st Summon.
On the 80th Summon, a Dimensional Hero is guaranteed, and the Summon guarantee count resets when a Hero appears.
If a Dimensional Hero you already own is Summoned, that Hero gains an Ascend effect.
If you obtain a duplicate Hero at max Ascend, you will receive 150 Dimensional Cores instead of an Ascend effect.

The appearance rate for Dimensional Heroes is 0.5%, and increases by 4% per Summon starting from the 61st Summon. On the 80th Summon, a Dimensional Hero is guaranteed, and the Summon guarantee count resets when a Hero appears. If a Dimensional Hero you already own is Summoned, that Hero gains an Ascend effect. If you obtain a duplicate Hero at max Ascend, you will receive 150 Dimensional Cores instead of an Ascend effect.
Use obstacles to secure a position where Neria is hard to attack from multiple directions.
Try using Neria's powerful Battle Start Skill!
In front of Vlad Moren's Mansion
The Battle Start Skill of Hero <color=#FFD145>{0}</color> has been unlocked.
You must reach Level 21 to use the Skill.
King Dimensional Hero Summon Scroll Package
God Dimensional Hero Summon Scroll Package
The one who wields the fastest sword in the East. The moment he draws his blade, all you feel is the cold mark on the back of your neck.
Spell DEF +{1} for every {0}% Spell Power gained from item or Relic/Accessory Secondary Stats
Upon taking damage, Taken damage {1}% for every {0}% difference in max HP compared to the target (max. {2}%)
After Battle starts, Crowd control resistance +{0}% and Spell DEF Penetration +{1}% every second (max at {2}s)
Upon taking damage, Taken damage {1}% for every {0}% difference in max HP compared to the target (max. {2}%)
Spell DEF +{1} for every {0}% Spell Power gained from item or Relic/Accessory Secondary Stats

At the start of Battle, Spell Power is fixed at {0}% of base Spell Power Effect multiplier by Tier: {1}/{2}/{3}/{4}x Spell DEF +{6} for every {5}% Spell Power gained from item or Relic/Accessory Secondary Stats

At the start of Battle, Spell Power is fixed at {0}% of base Spell Power Effect multiplier by Tier: {1}/{2}/{3}/{4}x Spell DEF +{6} for every {5}% Spell Power gained from item or Relic/Accessory Secondary Stats Upon taking damage, Taken damage {8}% for every {7}% difference in max HP compared to the target (max. {9}%) After Battle starts, Crowd control resistance +{10}% and Spell DEF Penetration +{11}% every second (max at {12}s)
Allies in <Support Fire> range gain additional Attack Speed equal to {Overcome:AttackSpeedPerUnit10790:DefDen:BaseValue}% of Guard
With each <Support Fire> bombardment, damage taken by allies in the area is limited to {Overcome:Guard}% of max HP for 1s
Instead, with each <Support Fire> bombardment, damage taken by allies in the area is limited to {Overcome:DescGuard}% of max HP for 1s
Allies in <Support Fire> range gain additional Attack Speed equal to {Overcome:AttackSpeedPerUnit10790:DefDen:BaseValue}% of Guard

Ophelia, who spent her life as a carefree young noblewoman lost in dance and wine, began to change after Roderick's death. Rumors say Ophelia has gone mad, but her wounded heart is only growing stronger.

Bombards the {SplashRangeX}{X}{SplashRangeY} area in front of the frontmost enemy in the same row for {107900@Time}s, dealing {10795@Damage} damage, slowing enemies, and increasing Attack Speed and Move Speed of allies in the area.

Every second for {107900@Time}s, uses Support Fire on the {SplashRangeX}{X}{SplashRangeY} area in front of the frontmost enemy in the same row. Each bombardment deals {10795@Damage} Spell Skill damage in the area, slows enemies by {1079005@Slow}% for {107905@Time}s, and increases the Attack Speed and Move Speed of allies in the area by {107905@AttackSpeedPerUnit10790:BaseValue}%. (For identical effects, only the highest value applies)

Bombards the {SplashRangeX}{X}{SplashRangeY} area in front of the frontmost enemy in the same row for {107900@Time}s, dealing {10795@Damage} damage, slowing enemies, and increasing Attack Speed and Move Speed of allies in the area.

Every second for {107900@Time}s, uses Support Fire on the {SplashRangeX}{X}{SplashRangeY} area in front of the frontmost enemy in the same row. Each bombardment deals {10795@Damage} Spell Skill damage in the area, slows enemies by {1079005@Slow}% for {107905@Time}s, and increases the Attack Speed and Move Speed of allies in the area by {107905@AttackSpeedPerUnit10790:BaseValue}%. Attack Speed additionally increases by {107905@AttackSpeedPerUnit10790:DefDen:BaseValue}% of Guard. (For identical effects, only the highest value applies)

Bombards the {SplashRangeX}{X}{SplashRangeY} area in front of the frontmost enemy in the same row for {107900@Time}s, dealing {10795@Damage} damage, slowing enemies, increasing Attack Speed and Move Speed of allies in the area, and limiting damage taken.

Every second for {107900@Time}s, uses Support Fire on the {SplashRangeX}{X}{SplashRangeY} area in front of the frontmost enemy in the same row. Each bombardment deals {10795@Damage} Spell Skill damage in the area, slows enemies by {1079005@Slow}% for {107905@Time}s, increases the Attack Speed and Move Speed of allies in the area by {107905@AttackSpeedPerUnit10790:BaseValue}%, and limits damage taken to {107905@Guard}% of max HP. Attack Speed additionally increases by {107905@AttackSpeedPerUnit10790:DefDen:BaseValue}% of Guard. (For identical effects, only the highest value applies)
At the start of the next Battle, all allies become Invincible for {0}s.
Incredibly fast learner in Dominion management and strategy
After Roderick's death, if nothing special is going on, she always spends time in Roderick's study.
Go to the lord in the South of the Empire to deliver the letter
Go to the place you figured out
Mel, over there—that letter. Aren't you curious what's in it? What if we open it just a little, then close it again?
Ugh... right? We shouldn't, right? But I'm so curious. Why did they trust us with it, and how does the lord in the South of the Empire know the old village chief?
Do you know this is already the fifth time you've said that?
Was it young love~? Rivals who fought back to back~? Dear friends who traveled together~? Ah! That's us right now!
You two, hold it right there. We've got something to check.
Is this road a toll road or something...?
Yeah! Not many people carry a sword like that.
Ladies, it's dangerous for two of you to walk a dark forest road like this. Want us to escort you?
Urgh... We can't beat them at all!
They're too strong! But we can't go back empty-handed...
We got one, now Tactical Retreat!
Huh?! What was that just now?
It's not too late yet. Come on, let's get it back!
Those bad Bandits are no match for our Mel!
Phew, they're fast. Like monkeys, phew. We lost them... Mel, what do we do now?
It's okay. The forest is thick, so they must have left tracks. Trampled ground, broken branches, things like that.
How do you know that stuff?
...Someone taught me a long time ago.
I think we should follow this trail.
But there are footprints over there, and there too, and over there too...
You can tell by how dry they are. This is the newest trail.
How dry they are...? Can you tell by touching them?
Mel! This looks like the bandana that Bandit was wearing earlier! Why is it torn?
There's Blood on it. He used it to stop the bleeding.
My heart is racing. I feel like a detective!
Several people often rested in this clearing.
There’s no moss on the tree up to a person’s height. They must have always put their bags down here.
This must have been a path people used often.
I’m not getting any answer. I heard lots of birds up to that path over there, but it’s strangely quiet here!
Ah, birds. Now that you mention it, I’ve heard that before. Birds avoid places where people pass by often. And this place isn’t even close to the village.
...But can you talk to birds too?
We understand each other with our hearts!
There are no signs of people here at all. Let’s look for more tracks somewhere else.
Hehe, I guess I was too hasty.
From the tracks we saw, it’s to the northeast. And I think we’re almost there.
Do you think there’s a bandit hideout? You know, the kind where the door opens when you say, “Open sesame”?
Yeah. Probably. There should be a place where they keep the things they stole.
Mel, you’re amazing! You’re good at fighting, and you’re good at detective work too.
I just did what my grandpa taught me.
Swordswoman or detective~ her name is Mel!
P-please be quiet. We might get spotted now.
Looks like they’re going to attack that wagon. I don’t think they’re the ones who took your Necklace.
Thank you, you saved us! They blocked our way out of nowhere, and we were in trouble.
Who are you two, anyway? Why did you help us?
We would like to repay you.
We were just passing by. No reward is needed. We have something urgent, so we need to go.
I’m Mel the swordswoman, and this is Bardrey the Bard!
But excuse me, which merchant group are you from?
We are the Rie merchant group.
Rie? I’ve never heard that name before.
Ahaha, we’re a brand-new merchant group. We just started trading, so you probably haven’t heard of us.

Mel, something’s strange. I’ve seen lots of merchant wagons, and no merchant wagon in the world would go down a narrow forest path with fine silk hanging outside for everyone to see. There’s no merchant crest, and there’s only one wagon.
Something’s strange! People like that are usually smugglers or black market dealers... or worse.
Aren’t you overthinking it? We need to get going.
Then let’s just check what they’re carrying. Will that make you feel better?
Commander Ren. They’re watching us strangely from over there.
Ask them to come with us. Confirm their identities and have them promise to keep silent.
Do we really have to go that far? They even beat the bandits, and they don’t seem like bad kids.
This is not about good intentions. We must keep the chance of mission failure as low as possible. Those children could be agents of a hostile force.
Aren’t you thinking too much into this?
Better too much than too little.
Excuse me! May I ask what kind of goods you’re carrying? Uh, uh? Why are you all suddenly...
Mel, Bardrey. I ask that you come with us for a moment. I promise you will not be harmed.
Mel. They all have weapons. You there! So you really are black market dealers or something, aren’t you? Are you trying to kidnap us?
This misunderstanding is only getting bigger. We are not bad people.
Don’t come any closer. If you have something to say, keep your distance and say it.
Show us inside the wagon first.
Where are you coming from, and where are you headed! Who are you people anyway!
I cannot tell you that either.
Not until we check the wagon.
If you come any closer, we will treat it as a hostile act.
See! They sound polite, but they’re total kidnappers!
No willingness to cooperate. Take them into custody. Avoid fatal blows.
I’m Mel. Thank you for helping us. But why did you help us?
I’m with the local watch... sort of. I keep an eye on the Bandits so they don’t harm civilians. Sometimes I watch from the trees too. That’s how I found you.
Hmm... You were watching us the whole time?
The people you were facing were Empire soldiers. If they catch you, it becomes a big hassle whether you did anything wrong or not, so it’s best to avoid them first.
So it really wasn’t a merchant wagon. If they were soldiers, why didn’t they just say so? Why were they acting so suspicious? Hmph.
It was probably an undercover mission.
So it was a misunderstanding. They weren’t bad people.
Not bad people... Well. They’re hard to judge as simply good or bad.
(So this is Mel. She has remarkable spirit.)
It looked like you were searching for something earlier. Do you need something?
The Bandits took something from us, so we were chasing them.
You two were chasing the Bandits by yourselves? Don’t do anything reckless and go back. If I find it, I’ll keep it safe for you. What exactly did you lose?
A Necklace. Can you find it?
Do you know where the Bandits’ hideout is? Please tell us.
Are you planning to go there yourself?
I have to get my Necklace back.
Mister, it’s really, really important to Mel. And you don’t need to worry. Mel is super strong.
(I want to help, but taking these children with me would add too many unknowns.)
It’s not a matter of being strong or weak...
I won’t give up until I get it back. Even if it’s just the two of us, we’ll go find it. Please, can’t you help us?
All right... Fine. I’ll help you, so come with me. But once we’re inside the hideout, move carefully so you don’t get spotted, and only take the Necklace and get out.
Do you know where the hideout is?
Weren’t we heading there right now?
You’ll know soon. The ones who ran away earlier left tracks, so we just have to follow them. If you look carefully, you can learn a lot.
Ah, Mel can do that too! She said you just follow the path where you can’t hear birds. Birds avoid places where people pass by a lot.
I never thought of that. Birds usually fly away when they hear gunshots. That’s a good idea.
But leave it to me here.
Aramis has the "Search" ability. When you use Search, hidden things are revealed and you can interact with them.
A tree is broken. It looks like the Bandits rushed through here. Then that means their hideout is right this way.
These aren't the ones who took your necklace.
When a branch breaks on this tree, sticky sap comes out. At first it is thin and runs down, but over time it hardens. For it to get this hard, more than 8 hours must have passed.
Hmph, we almost followed the wrong trail. Sap...? Ugh, it's sticky!
Did you really have to touch it with your hand?
You said how thick it is matters.

There are six of them in total. One person has unusually large feet and is heavy. One person has trouble with the left foot. This is a scratch mark from a blade dragging on the ground. A curved blade often used by Bandits.
One big person, and one with a bad left foot... I think they really are the Bandits we met at the start, right?
There are so many footprints. How can you tell all that?
Because it's a muddy road, the details of the footprints stayed clear. You have to look at foot size, stride length, and pressure distribution together.
What a huge spider web. Do you think a giant spider made it...? No one came through this way, right?
Hm. The spider that makes this kind of web is the Red-Spotted Empire Spider, and it spins its web at dawn. No one has passed through here since dawn.

Aramis takes a neatly folded paper from his coat. When he opens it, a hand-drawn map appears. It is as neat as if it were printed, but so complex that it is hard to understand. Aramis draws a few lines on the map.
(30 degrees northeast from here, margin of error 3 degrees. 103 degrees southeast from here, margin of error 3 degrees. I checked this section last time, and—)
Mary, I found it. Reliability 98%, 320 meters from our current location.
Ah. Sorry. I was too focused and got it wrong.
(I can't rule out something like large-scale illusion magic, so strictly speaking I can't say it's 100%...)

Wait. Mary, no, Mel. Don't forget, we agreed to sneak in carefully without being seen. Bandits are experts at grabbing things and carrying them off. If they spot us and run away, this will get troublesome.
Find Mel's necklace in the Bandits' hideout
Mel, I know this is serious.
But I'm so excited. I feel like the hero of a spy novel I read when I was little!
Yeah. I'll definitely find your necklace!
(The guards are sloppier than I thought. Getting in will be easy, but there may be more of them deeper inside.)
If we get spotted, don't panic. Stay calm, split up, and come back to this spot. Understood?
Mel, Bardrey. There seem to be more guards from here on.
Stay out of the guards' sight, and the moving guards may have fixed routes, so watch them for a while first.
Bardrey, you need to spend as little time as possible outside the blind spot. If you move that slowly, it won't work.
I couldn't help it if I wanted to move quietly. Your rifle keeps bumping into things and going clunk, clunk when you move.
What, my gun was making noise? I didn't know. Sorry.
How much is all this worth? This is a huge group of bad guys...
This rifle is a military model used by the Empire Army...
Did they even steal from the Empire Army?
Necklaces, rings, hmm, yours isn't here, Mel...
Is this a key? It isn't rusty, so it looks like it's used often.
Star of Twilight. Test subject Mara. Stable after 30 minutes in the 3rd experiment. Repeated exposure increases the stable time. Soon we may be able to get a perfect Mara...?
What is the Star of Twilight?
It's what the Bandits are after, but I don't know the details either.
Star of Twilight, Star of Twilight. Pretty name...
Star of Twilight usually means the bright star that appears first in the evening sky after sunset. It usually refers to Venus. Strictly speaking, Venus is not a star but a planet.
You're right. The other tiles sound dull, but this one sounds hollow? Is there space underneath?
Names of nobles? An Empire Army division commander. A deputy finance minister.
So the officials were taking bribes from the Bandits...! That's why the Bandits were able to operate so openly.
I feel like we learned something we were not supposed to know...
What do you keep putting away? Are you stealing from the Bandits?
It's part of the militia's work.
Right, if there's a key, there must be a matching keyhole.
This room looks like a study. It's really small, though.
(A letter. Capturing the 'Chosen One' is the top priority? This is...!)
What is it? Why did you get startled and hide it?
For a second, I thought there was a necklace.
I don't even see anything that looks like a necklace. Was it an interesting letter? Show me too.
I hear someone approaching from outside. Let's go, quickly.
Only this room is locked? That's suspicious...
What kind of room is this...? It's kind of creepy.
I finally found it... What a relief...
I knew we'd find it quickly! What a relief.
You went to a lot of trouble to come all the way to this shabby place.
But isn't it rude for guests to point such a dangerous gun at me first?
Hmm, so you're Mel? Nice to meet you.
Why are you so attached to that necklace? Is it expensive? Heh, does it have some special story behind it?
I just had to wave that thing around, and you came to me on your own. What do they call that again? A fish biting the bait?
Mara, you look especially well today, hm?
This wasn't part of the plan.

Mara's body staggers. Bellina's eyes shine, and she waves her hand once. As if a puppet's strings were cut, Mara drops to the floor. At the same time, the other three freeze as if vines had wrapped around them.
That singer is quite a nuisance. Not many can block my voice.
Mara. You're fine, right? Of course you are. It's time to show your skill.

The light fades from Bellina's eyes. Freed from the binding, Mel and Aramis take up a battle stance. Mel raises her sword, and Aramis aims his gun. Black energy pours out from Mara, who stands there like a living corpse. It was like human malice gathered into one mass.
My gun is completely ruined. Oh no!
Mel, are you okay? What do we do?
What do I do? What should I do?
I can... still fight. Let me go.
Check on the three civilians. Suppress the clawed woman and the leader of the Bandits.
You okay? I told you we aren't the bad guys.
Th- Thank you. Mel, drink this.
Works right away, doesn't it? Even after fighting for 3 days and nights, one of these will get you right back on your feet.
My, using numbers to overwhelm me is cheating. How crude.
Huh? My body is moving on its own! Watch out!
Ugh. Does she have the power to control bodies?!

Bellina toyed with the soldiers of the Empire, stealing control of their bodies and giving it back again to spread chaos. They swung their swords at each other, tripped over their own legs, and even punched themselves in the face. It was Rossette's Charge that cleared the chaos in an instant.
Hahaha, I hear that a lot!
So it's mind magic. It tingles, but it doesn't work on me. A healthy body makes a healthy mind!
This little creature's body is not mine.
My body feels stiffer than usual!
In the middle of the chaotic battlefield, Aramis focuses on what he can do: contacting the reinforcements through a communication artifact.
Hah, hah, please tell us the situation inside in as much detail as you can.
From the door... at 40m... the wall material is...
A thin song rises. The oddly sorrowful voice cutting into the fierce battle covers Bellina's voice of domination. Two wills lock in a fierce tug-of-war, forming another battlefield.
The battle is over! Block the escape route!

Mara, who had been lying on the ground, suddenly jumps back up. That body, already brought down twice, looked like a torn rag and gave off an ominous air. Moving like a spider, she rushed toward Bellina in an instant. A hidden switch on the wall is pressed, and a vertical passage is revealed.
Memories will become shackles, and bonds will become blades.
What you longed for and pursued will bring the exact opposite result,
and only then will the Void swallow you.
Until we meet again, Mel, and you too, singer.
Boss. Want me to go into that passage? Feels like if I go down, I won't be coming back up.
No. We'll leave that for now. More importantly, take the three civilians into custody.
The Star of Twilight has been destroyed for good, so the operation has failed. We need to find out what that sword is that destroyed the Star of Twilight.
Wait! We're on the same side!
We were never on the same side. You two already escaped once. This time, please follow our procedure.
Right on target. Not off by even an inch!
Bombie, now is not the time.
Mel, Bardrey. They're on our side.
Identify yourself. There shouldn't be any Bandits left.
I am the Shadow of the Empire. For now, I have lost my name.
Rena Edir Junipe of House Junipe.
A memory flashes through Ren's mind.
Ren recalls the Leonhardt she once saw from afar when she had just joined the army of the Empire. He was called the bravest Shield of the Empire.
There was a time when Ren wanted to be like him.
And on the day the Emperor was assassinated, an official notice named Leonhardt as the culprit.
You've changed so much I almost didn't recognize you.
Who was the superior who ordered the operation to recover the Star of Twilight?
Do you even know what the Star of Twilight is?
It's an artifact filled with the power of the Void. A dangerous object that should not exist in this world.
You were ordered to "recover" it, not destroy it. Do you know why?
Have you ever questioned it, even once?
A ghost like you thinks it can test my loyalty with words.
Without even knowing why, you can slowly stray onto the wrong path, be used completely, and then be thrown away in misery. Junipe, that can happen to you too.
So face it clearly. Where your path is leading.
I am a tool of the Empire. I serve only the Empire.
Hah. Then we'll need magic tracking.
-He was thrown away so miserably. Junipe, the same could happen to you. So look closely at where your path is leading.
-I changed the bomb mixture a little based on the wall material you told me about. But on the side where the Empire soldiers were-
-Wasn't the blast power a little too low? I don't think you accounted for the humidity.
Ah, you're right. I didn't think of that part. The relative humidity in that area was a bit high. Then I need to adjust the mixture - Whew, I still have a long way to go.
No, Bombie! I just mean it as helpful feedback. You were truly amazing today. Without you, we would have been helpless-
Ahaha, really? Come to think of it, about the fuse coating-

Uncle Leonhardt used something called a Scroll of Teleportation to help us escape. Then you collapsed right away. I was so scared! I thought something happened to you. They say that can happen if you Teleport when you're really, really tired.
Oh, we're riding in a carriage to see the Lord of the South right now. Uncle Leonhardt and the others also have business with him, so we're traveling together.
Mel... I'm really, really glad you're okay. When that scary claw monster charged at us, I thought it was all over...
There was nothing I could do... That made me so sad...
I was so afraid I might lose you...
It's okay now. That will never happen again.
And it's not true that there was nothing you could do, Bardrey. I heard your voice the whole time we were fighting. Because you didn't give up and kept singing for me, I was able to keep going too.
The reason I made it through safely and got the Necklace back was because you were there, Bardrey.
Alright! I've cried enough. Let's get going again!
But your eyes are still all puffy.
No! So many amazing things happened today, so I need to turn them into a song before I forget. Don't talk to me now!
Leonhardt... sir? I heard you saved us. Thank you.
Well, it just worked out that way. I didn't do it to be thanked. More importantly, Mel.
Hahaha, I see. I heard from your friend about what happened so far. You beat Goblins, saved people, took down the Bandits, and even carry a shining sword, but you're not a hero.
I only did what I had to do.
Right. You'll understand someday that fate is real. More than that, you said you were bringing a letter to the Lord of the South, right? Where are you going after that?
I'm going to the Central Kingdom.
Perfect. Then we can travel together. We're heading to the Central Kingdom too.
That Lord of the South is an expert at getting people past the Empire checkpoints and into the Central Kingdom.
But there is something I want to ask. What kind of work do you and Mr. Aramis do? Mr. Aramis said he was with a militia.
Did you say you were with a militia?
That is not too far from the truth, is it?
Hahaha, I stopped thinking about public good and justice a long time ago. Mel, we're the Resistance.
Are we allowed to tell her?

It's an organization of people who are unfairly oppressed and harmed by those in power. They band together and help others in the same situation. But that's not all. Something no one understands is happening in the Empire right now. And the Star of Twilight is tied to it.

I do work as part of the Resistance, but I am also a knight of the Central Kingdom. I was given a mission by the Commander-in-Chief to find you, Mel. That is why I helped you. I'm sorry I kept it hidden.
No. I should be the one thanking you. There was nothing you needed to apologize for.
But the Central Kingdom... How does the Commander-in-Chief know me? Why is he looking for me?
I'll tell you the rest later. Either way, we're still going to the Central Kingdom together.
Deliver the letter to the Lord
Same as ever. Living well by taking everything I can from my people.
Well now, as a member of the Resistance, I cannot let that pass.
How did the operation go this time?
It was a total mess. Bellina escaped, we got tangled up with the Empire army, and the Star of Twilight was destroyed.
But we found something valuable instead. Important documents were scattered around the Bandits' hideout. I brought them all back safely.
There are financial ties between the Bandits and some nobles and leaders of the Empire.
The leader of the Bandits was acting under orders from someone connected to the Empire's royal family.
And there is Lena Edir Junipe from the Empire army. We need to find out who his superior is. I have a feeling he is connected to the one behind all this.
You got involved with the Empire army? You are not planning to dump another troublesome job on me and run off to the Central Kingdom, are you?
The funding for the Resistance is going smoothly. You do not need to worry.
You do not need to thank me. I am doing this because I believe it is the right path for my people and for the Empire.
U-um, may I ask what was in the letter? I've been so curious from the start.
Of course. I will read it for you.

'Hey, old friend. Are you doing well? Something big happened in my village this time, but thanks to a girl named Mel, we all stayed safe. Please repay that kindness for me. She needs to get to the Central Kingdom, so take care of the checkpoints and the travel pass for her. I will count this against the unpaid beef bill from 5 years ago. Oh, and bring that 7-year-old wine from your Dominion too. I have been waiting a long time. That is all.'
You two must be old friends.

More like old rivals. It has been 45 years. Every year he keeps telling me to come visit him. But think about it. Who is busier, a lord or a village chief? Of course he should be the one coming to me.
Your ride is ready. I have taken care of the travel pass and the checkpoints, so just leave without worry. You will depart by carriage in 1 hour.
Thank you for taking care of this.
Then let's set off for the Central Kingdom!
